briefs.title: Burnaby Council approves rezoning, housing, and infrastructure contracts at March 24 meeting
briefs.summary: At the March 24, 2026 meeting, Burnaby City Council voted on several items including rezoning applications for mixed-use and self-storage developments, a $4.2M water metering contract, hired equipment and paving contracts, and endorsed a Poverty Reduction Strategy. A modular housing pilot project was sent back to staff for further review, and three bylaws covering parking meters, fees, and bylaw enforcement received final adoption.
briefs.action_required: If your business is near 6535 Kingsway, 2757 Norland Ave, or 5058 Imperial St, monitor upcoming public hearings on rezoning bylaws.
briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Transportation & Logistics

briefs.title: Council to Consider Letter Urging Federal Action on Chlorine Rail Transport Safety
briefs.summary: At a Special Council meeting on April 1, 2026, Burnaby Council voted unanimously to have Mayor Hurley write a letter to the Federal Minister of Transport raising serious safety concerns about liquid chlorine being transported by rail through densely populated areas of the Lower Mainland. The concern stems from a nearby chlorine plant in North Vancouver that ships an average of four rail cars of liquid chlorine through Burnaby every day — a risk that presenters say has not been fully assessed in a pending bylaw amendment before North Vancouver's council.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the District of North Vancouver's April 13 council vote — it could affect chlorine rail traffic through Burnaby.

key_dates.label: District of North Vancouver — Chemtrade chlorine bylaw amendment third reading

briefs.title: Council to Consider: Declaring April 28th Day of Mourning for Workers
briefs.summary: At its April 14, 2026 meeting, Burnaby City Council will consider a request from the New Westminster & District Labour Council to officially declare April 28th as the 'Day of Mourning for Workers Killed and Injured on the Job.' Council will also be asked to observe a minute of silence in chambers and encourage city staff to do the same at 11:00 a.m. on April 28th.
briefs.action_required: Mark April 28th on your calendar — consider observing a minute of silence with your staff at 11:00 a.m.

key_dates.label: Burnaby Council meeting — Day of Mourning delegation presentation
Day of Mourning for Workers — proposed Burnaby observance date

briefs.title: Council to Vote on New Chief Election Officer for Burnaby's 2026 Municipal Election
briefs.summary: Burnaby Council is set to vote on appointing Dennis Back as Chief Election Officer for the October 17, 2026 City of Burnaby General Local Election. The original appointee is on leave and not expected to return in time, so Council will consider replacing her with Back, a seasoned administrator who previously served as Burnaby's Municipal Clerk.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — this affects who will run Burnaby's 2026 municipal election.

key_dates.label: Burnaby 2026 General Local Election date

briefs.title: Council to Consider: North Road BIA Renewal Blocked by Sufficient Owner Opposition
briefs.summary: Council is set to receive a report showing that enough property owners on North Road voted against renewing the Business Improvement Area (BIA) — a special tax zone where businesses pay extra fees to fund local improvements. Because 55% of property owners (representing 52% of total property value) submitted opposition petitions, the proposed renewal bylaw cannot move forward, and the North Road BIA will not receive funding for 2026–2027.
briefs.action_required: If you are a North Road BIA member, watch for updates on a possible new renewal attempt in fall 2026.

key_dates.label: Burnaby North Road BIA renewal bylaw — Council consideration meeting

briefs.title: Council to vote on Burnaby Housing Authority annual governance and board appointments for 2026
briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on routine annual housekeeping matters for the Burnaby Housing Authority Corporation (BHA) — a city-owned housing company. If approved, Council would skip a formal annual general meeting (AGM), appoint KPMG as auditor, confirm December 12, 2026 as the official reference date, accept the BHA's 2025 financial statements, and formally appoint 10 directors to the BHA board for two- or three-year terms.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the Council decision on April 14, 2026 — this affects Burnaby's city-owned housing corporation governance.

key_dates.label: BHA annual reference date — Burnaby Housing Authority 2026 AGM waiver takes effect

briefs.industries: Landlord & Property Mgmt, Construction & Development, Finance & Insurance

briefs.title: Council to consider: Burnaby Housing Authority 2025 financials and 2026 operating budget
briefs.summary: Council is set to receive the Burnaby Housing Authority's (BHA) first-ever audited financial statements and its 2026 operating budget of $1.96 million. Accepting these documents is the final condition required before the City can release $2 million in Year 2 operating funding to the BHA, which is actively developing 387 new rental homes across two Burnaby sites.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — it unlocks $2M in housing authority funding affecting local rental housing supply.

key_dates.label: BHA Year 2 operating funding release — council receipt meeting date
BHA GIC investment matures — $25M cash event

briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Finance & Insurance

briefs.title: Council to vote on bylaw changes for e-bike share and e-scooter programs in Burnaby
briefs.summary: Burnaby Council is set to vote on amendments to three city bylaws that would set the rules for a new shared e-bike rental program and bring the city in line with BC's provincial e-scooter pilot. The proposed changes would define where e-bikes and electric kick scooters can be ridden and parked, create designated parking zones for shared devices, and set fines of $200 for riding in areas not approved for these vehicles.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — new riding and parking rules for e-bikes and e-scooters could affect your business.

key_dates.label: Burnaby Council meeting — e-bike share and e-scooter bylaw amendments vote

briefs.title: Council to vote on submitting Burnaby's first provincial housing target progress report
briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on authorizing staff to submit Burnaby's first Housing Target Progress Report to the BC Ministry of Housing and Municipal Affairs. The province has ordered Burnaby to build 10,240 net new homes by August 2030, and this report covers the first six months of that period (Sept 1, 2025 – Feb 28, 2026), during which approximately 910 net new units were completed. While Burnaby is currently on track for Year 1, slowing construction starts and a pause in provincial affordable housing funding are raising concerns about meeting future targets.
briefs.action_required: If you develop or own rental property in Burnaby, monitor upcoming housing policy changes that could affect your projects.

key_dates.label: Burnaby Housing Target Progress Report — Year 1b submission deadline to province
Burnaby Development Permit system (Form & Character, Streamside, Tenant Protection) takes effect
Burnaby Tenant Protection Bylaw — expected Council consideration

briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Tenant

briefs.title: Council to Vote on Burnaby Rejoining Building Benchmark BC Energy Tracking Program
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on whether to continue the city's participation in Building Benchmark BC (BBBC) for Year 7 (2026). If approved, the city would keep tracking and comparing energy use and carbon emissions for 30 city-owned buildings, promote the program to private building owners, and contribute roughly $16,500 — all within the existing Planning and Development budget.
briefs.action_required: If you own a commercial or multi-family building in Burnaby, watch for the city's BBBC recruitment campaign — joining is free.

key_dates.label: Burnaby Council vote — Building Benchmark BC Year 7 participation approval

briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Professional Services

briefs.title: Council to vote on: FortisBC natural gas access rights on Burnaby Mountain City land
briefs.summary: Burnaby Council is set to vote on granting FortisBC Energy Inc. a Statutory Right-of-Way (SRW) — a legal permission allowing a utility to use a strip of someone else's land — over portions of two City-owned properties at 7950 Pandora St. and 1200 Gaglardi Way. If approved, this would let FortisBC install, operate, and maintain natural gas infrastructure needed to service the Mintara restaurant and other facilities in the Burnaby Mountain Conservation Area.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the April 14, 2026 council decision — no public hearing is scheduled for this item.

key_dates.label: Burnaby Council vote — FortisBC Burnaby Mountain SRW approval

briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Natural Resources, Food Service

briefs.title: Council to vote on $1.35M contract for modular four-plex housing pilot in Burnaby
briefs.summary: Burnaby Council is set to vote on awarding a $1,354,463 contract to Echelon Creative Inc. to build a modular (factory-built) four-unit housing building at 8315 14th Avenue as a pilot project. The three-storey building would be owned by the City and rented out, with the goal of testing whether this style of construction is a cost-effective way to add small-scale housing in lower-density neighbourhoods.
briefs.action_required: Watch the April 14, 2026 council meeting decision — this pilot could shape future modular housing development rules in Burnaby.

key_dates.label: Burnaby Council vote — modular four-plex contract award decision

briefs.title: Council to vote on $2.4M contract for Jerry Rogers Creek culvert replacement in Burnaby
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on awarding a $2,404,076 contract to HANNA Infrastructure Ltd. to replace an undersized culvert at Jerry Rogers Creek. The project would install a new box culvert built to handle major flood events, along with upgrades to nearby water and sewer lines.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the April 14, 2026 council decision if your business operates near Jerry Rogers Creek or Marine Drive.

key_dates.label: Burnaby Council vote — Jerry Rogers Creek culvert contract award

briefs.title: Council to vote on $1.36M contract for Central Valley Greenway culvert and paving work
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on awarding a $1,360,391 contract to Drake Excavating (2016) Ltd. to replace 16 culverts (underground drainage pipes) and repave the Central Valley Greenway trail between Sperling Avenue and Douglas Road. The work is already budgeted in the City's 2026–2030 Financial Plan, with funding split between City reserves and TransLink.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the April 14, 2026 council decision if your business operates near the Central Valley Greenway trail.

key_dates.label: Burnaby Council vote — Central Valley Greenway culvert contract award

briefs.title: Council to vote on $450,000 contract change to keep Rogers cellular services running
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on a proposed contract change worth $450,000 (including taxes) to keep Rogers Communications Canada Inc. providing cellular voice and data services to the City on a month-to-month basis. This is a temporary measure — a bridge — while the City finishes negotiating a new contract with a different provider selected through a public bidding process.
briefs.action_required: Watch the April 14, 2026 Council meeting decision — this is a City contract with no direct compliance impact on most businesses.

briefs.title: Council to vote on $579,600 contract change for Burnaby city building HVAC services
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on a contract change worth $579,600 (including taxes) with Just Mechanical Ltd. to keep heating, ventilation, and air conditioning (HVAC) systems running in city-owned buildings. This is a short-term extension to avoid any gap in service while the City runs a new competitive bidding process to find a long-term provider.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision on April 14, 2026 — this affects HVAC service contractors bidding on city work.

key_dates.label: Burnaby Council meeting — HVAC contract change vote
Just Mechanical interim HVAC contract end date — Burnaby

briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby Council to Vote on $394,942 Cisco WebEx Contact Center License Renewal
briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on renewing a three-year software license subscription with Long View Systems Corporation for approximately $394,942 (taxes included). This renewal would keep Burnaby's Customer Service Centre phone system running for city residents and staff from May 5, 2026 to May 4, 2029.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the April 14, 2026 council decision — no direct action required for most business owners.

key_dates.label: Burnaby Council vote on Cisco WebEx Contact Center license renewal
Cisco WebEx Contact Center renewal term start date — if approved

briefs.title: Council to vote on $1.67M fire protection services contract renewal — Burnaby
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on renewing a contract with A.W. Fireguard Supplies (1991) Ltd. to inspect, maintain, and repair fire protection and emergency lighting systems at City-owned buildings. The proposed three-year renewal would run from July 1, 2026 to June 30, 2029, at an estimated cost of $1,666,061 including taxes.
briefs.action_required: If you supply fire protection services, watch this contract — it signals Burnaby's procurement cycle for these services.

key_dates.label: Burnaby fire protection services contract renewal start date
Burnaby fire protection services contract renewal end date

briefs.title: Council to vote on hiring an outside expert to review Burnaby's financial practices
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on hiring an independent (outside) financial reviewer to look at how the City plans its budget, manages day-to-day spending, and decides which big projects to fund. The review is meant to help protect the City's financial health and reduce the risk of larger tax increases or rising service costs for businesses and residents.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the April 14, 2026 council decision — findings could influence future tax and service cost changes.

key_dates.label: Burnaby Council vote — external financial review approval

briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby Council to Consider Rezoning 5035 Beresford St for 20-Storey Rental Tower
briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on advancing a rezoning application that would allow a 20-storey rental apartment building with 215 units to be built at 5035 Beresford Street in Burnaby's Royal Oak neighbourhood. The site currently holds a printing shop, and the proposed bylaw would change its zoning from industrial use to a residential development district — meaning the land rules that govern what can be built there would be updated to allow this apartment project.
briefs.action_required: Monitor Burnaby Council meeting agendas for the First and Second Reading vote date on this rezoning bylaw.

key_dates.label: Burnaby REZ #25-11 rezoning bylaw — First and Second Reading council meeting

briefs.title: Council to Consider: Update on Mayor's Task Force on Burnaby Economic Growth
briefs.summary: Council will consider an update on the Mayor's Task Force on Burnaby Economic Growth and Resilience, a group of business and community leaders working to identify ways to strengthen Burnaby's local economy. The Task Force is still in progress — it plans to deliver final recommendations to Council on June 23, 2026, covering topics like supporting businesses at every stage, reducing barriers for underrepresented groups, and building economic resilience.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the Task Force's final recommendations to Council on June 23, 2026 — they may affect local business support programs.

key_dates.label: Burnaby Economic Growth Task Force — final recommendations presented to Council

briefs.title: Council to Receive Update: Developer Forfeits $1.36M Instead of Building Fraser River Pier
briefs.summary: Council will consider an information report about Oxford Properties' decision to forfeit (give up) $1,363,500 in security money rather than build a planned public pier on the Fraser River at Riverbend Business Park. The developer could not get the required provincial land approval to build on the river, so under a legal agreement already in place, they are paying the City instead. Council will decide later how to spend those funds on public amenities.
briefs.action_required: Watch for future Council decisions on how the $1.36M will be spent on public amenities near the Fraser Foreshore.

briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby North Road BIA Bylaw Would Fund Area Improvements Over Five Years
briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a proposed bylaw that would formally establish the Burnaby North Road Business Improvement Area (BIA) — a defined zone where businesses pool resources to improve the area — and approve up to $1,755,147.81 in grants to the Burnaby North Road Business Association from April 2026 to March 2031. If passed, property owners in the BIA with light industrial or commercial properties would pay a special local tax to fund streetscape improvements, graffiti removal, and business promotion.
briefs.action_required: Check if your property falls within the Burnaby North Road BIA boundary — you may owe a new local tax.

key_dates.label: Burnaby North Road BIA bylaw — proposed start date takes effect
Burnaby North Road BIA — annual budget submission deadline (Association)
Burnaby North Road BIA — first annual grant payment deadline
Burnaby North Road BIA bylaw — proposed five-year term ends

briefs.industries: Retail, Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt
